title: grants — rollout flags
description: Rollout / change-management flags for THIS module. Distinct from `features.json` (business-tier features gated by entitlement + plan). Every material change ships behind a flag per SOC 2 CC8.1.

flags.description: Master kill-switch. Off = every write endpoint returns 503 without a redeploy. Existing grants continue to resolve normally through GrantOverlayResolver — the switch only blocks NEW grants. Incident-response only.

Master switch for GrantOverlayResolver participation in access/rbac's PermissionResolver. Off = every check falls through to RBAC-only (deny disappears, all allow grants disappear, delegation deny-check disappears). Incident-response only — turning this off leaves grant rows in the DB but they're not enforced. Alerts on-call every 60s while off.
Toggle for the `grants.warm_grant_cache` middleware. Off = resolver falls back to per-check DB lookups (slower but correct). Used during incidents where cache warming causes memory pressure.

Rollout guard for the deny-decision issue path. Off by default; enterprise entitlement flips on. Provides an extra kill-switch beyond the entitlement so we can freeze deny writes tenant-wide during an incident.
Rollout guard for the CSV bulk-issue endpoint. Independent of the entitlement so ops can freeze bulk writes if the BulkGrantIssueJob is misbehaving without pulling the tenant's plan.

cohorts.name: enterprise-with-workflow
cohorts.predicate: tenant.subscription.plan = 'enterprise' AND tenant.modules.workflow_approvals = 'active' AND tenant.entitlements.cross_role_grants = true

flags.description: Rollout guard for the cross-role grant escape hatch (grantor grants a permission not in their role, gated by an approval flow). Enterprise + workflow/approvals co-active.
Rollout guard for the L2 Redis evaluation cache. Off = L1 in-process only (per-worker; cache-miss on cold worker). Used during Redis latency incidents.

Rollout guard for the ReconcileOrphanedGrantsJob (the auto-revoke path when a resource disappears out-of-band). Off in v1 — dry-run scheduled entry still emits the metric so ops can see would-be revokes. Canary bucket enables the write path for one tenant per canary group.
Rollout guard for the periodic access-review workflow (ISO 27001 A.9.2.5). Off in Wave 1b — surface exists in features.json but the scheduler + notification wiring lands in a later wave.
Rollout guard for the platform-admin audit-report endpoint. On by default — gated by the enterprise entitlement anyway. Kill-switch during platform incidents.

retirement_policy.description: A flag lives at most 90 days after reaching 100% GA. After 90d, either the flag is deleted OR converts to a `kill-switch`. `grants:review-flags` command prints ages monthly (schedule.json entry).
